description: Pull saved memories, custom instructions, projects, and recurring preferences out of ChatGPT, Claude, or Gemini in one paste. Behavioral preferences (tone, voice, format) append to the user's profile so they load every session. Contextual content (projects, memories, decisions) lands in imports and surfaces via INDEX when relevant. Raises to org or user.md are explicit, never automatic. Standalone — resolves the user's handle from the conversation surface; no other package required.

customize.description: Where imported context lands by default. Resolved per-user under the user's brain folder unless overridden.

changelog.notes: Standalone identity: drop the add-roles-permissions prerequisite and the team-member-memory whoami-resolver dependency. Both of those use cases are hidden from the library while they're reworked, so nothing shown may require or route users to them. The skill now derives the handle from the conversation surface itself (Slack profile email or Motion userEmail, local part slugified; asks once if no email is available), files under /agent/brain/users/<handle>/, states the handle and path in the Phase 4 restate-and-gate so the user can correct it before the first write, and stops rather than writing into a folder that clearly belongs to someone else. If add-roles-permissions is installed later, its stricter resolvers govern — handles derived the same way stay compatible.

Convert post-install intro from passive nudge into an active extraction-flow trigger. Install now closes by asking which AI the user is importing from (ChatGPT, Claude, or Gemini); answering enters SKILL.md Phase 1 immediately and hands over the matching extraction prompt with paste-and-upload instructions. Removes the 'Try this now' list since the same path is now reachable in one answer. Closes the gap where users were installing the skill but never returning to trigger the flow.
Added post-install intro. After install completes, Runneth surfaces a short 'what just opened up + try this now' message sourced from post-install-intro.md.

Initial release. Single-shot extraction prompts for ChatGPT, Claude, and Gemini. Inline triage assigns each item a bucket (behavioral or contextual): behavioral items auto-append to the user's profile so they load every session; contextual items live in imports and surface via INDEX. Raises to org or user.md are explicit. Read-only HTML review with chat-based approval grammar.

post-install.name: Introduce the new capability
post-install.description: Read post-install-intro.md from this use case folder. After all prior install and post-install steps complete, surface the file's sections to the user in chat as the closing message of the install turn: '## What just opened up' as prose, '## Compounds with' only when present and at least one named use case is installed in the sandbox, then '## Start now' as the final line — verbatim — including the provider question. Do not rephrase the question or skip it. If the user's next message names a provider (ChatGPT, Claude, or Gemini), enter SKILL.md Phase 1 immediately for that provider and hand over the matching prompt from prompts/<provider>.md with paste-and-upload instructions. If the user says skip or moves on, leave the import dormant — the behavior snippet will pick it up when they trigger it later. Resolve any {{TOKEN}} placeholders using the same customize map applied during install.
